Off Calendar Mar-18-2026 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Each Of The Two Beneficiaries Of The Firearms Filing Proof Of A Valid Firearm Safety Certificate And Report Of Operation Of Law Having Been Received By The Doj
Off Calendar Mar-18-2026 Continued To Apr-15-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Christian Jajeh for executor John Nazzal, who was also present (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on: 1) Status of Filing a Valid Firearm Safety Certificate; 2) Order to Show Cause; and 3) Order to Appear.

The Court noted that the required compliance documents had not been filed and were not reflected in the e-filing system. Mr. Jajeh reported that the documents had not been filed due to an issue with the e-filing system and stated they would be filed promptly.

The Court continued the matter to April 15, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The Order to Show Cause and Order to Appear were taken off calendar.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Each Of The Two Beneficiaries Of The Firearms Filing Proof Of A Valid Firearm Safety Certificate And Report Of Operation Of Law Having Been Received By The Doj
Off Calendar Jan-21-2026 Continued To Mar-18-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The matter was called for hearing on status of filing a valid firearm safety certificate. No party or counsel appeared.

The Court continued the matter to March 18, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and stated that an Order to Appear and Order to Show Cause shall issue to attorney Christian R. Jajeh and executor John Nazzal, requiring counsel to explain why the required firearm safety documentation has not been filed and why sanctions in the amount of $250 should not be imposed for failure to appear.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition To Confirm Title To Estate Property And Determine Entitlement To Estate Distribution Based On Consents
Off Calendar Jun-26-2024 Continued To Jul-02-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Sep-05-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

This matter was put on for hearing with no appearance required for the purpose of admitting the will. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated June 13, 2020 is admitted to probate. John Nazzal is appointed as the Executor of the estate with full authority. Bond is not required. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for September 17, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If either a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status on administration is filed two weeks prior to the hearing date, the status hearing will go off calendar. Michael Kasolas is appointed Probate Referee.

Judge: Cecilia Castellanos. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Yang. Not reported.
